items.mistake: Adding every available column because 'more data is better'
items.fix: Start from domain-critical features; add columns only when validation improves; remove or aggregate noise dimensions.
items.why: Irrelevant dimensions increase d without signal, worsening sparsity and distance concentration — effective local sample size per feature direction drops.
items.example: Churn model: 500 hashed URL tokens + 10 behavioral metrics → k-NN AUC falls vs 10-metric baseline alone.

items.mistake: Running k-NN on raw high-dimensional embeddings without checking neighbor stability
items.fix: Inspect distance histograms, try PCA or smaller d, compare to linear or tree baselines on the same split.
items.why: When max/min distance ratio nears 1, neighbor rankings are mostly noise — accuracy reflects concentration, not bad hyperparameter k alone.
items.example: 784-D MNIST pixels: 1-NN ~3% error on train, poor test until PCA to 50-D.

items.mistake: Expecting linear sample-size growth to fix high-d local learning
items.fix: Plan for exponential coverage needs, reduce intrinsic dimension, or switch to models with stronger global structure (linear + regularization, trees).
items.why: Grid coverage scales as r^d; doubling n does not compensate when d jumps from 10 to 100.
items.example: 10 bins/axis in d=20 needs 10^20 cells — 10k rows cannot densely cover that grid.

items.mistake: Treating curse of dimensionality as proof that PCA always helps
items.fix: Validate reduced pipelines on held-out data; if labels depend on low-variance directions, use supervised selection or alternative reductions.
items.why: PCA keeps high-variance directions, which may be noise; curse explains pressure to reduce d, not which directions to keep.
items.example: Rare fraud flag variance tiny but predictive — PCA(10) drops it; supervised feature ranking retains it.

items.mistake: Ignoring feature correlation when citing distance concentration
items.fix: Examine effective rank, correlation matrix, and intrinsic dimension estimates; correlated features do not add independent volume like i.i.d. coordinates.
items.why: Concentration formulas for i.i.d. uniform coordinates overstate the curse when features are redundant or manifold-like.
items.example: Duplicate column copied twice: d counts 2 but intrinsic dimension unchanged — scaling hurts without duplicate removal.
